name: Pontón de la Oliva to La Parra Dam Circular Trail
name_original: Ruta de la Presa de La Parra – circular por el Pontón de la Oliva
description: Hike the moderate 10.6-mile Pontón de la Oliva to La Parra Dam Circular Trail, exploring historic dams and river canyon views.
region_intro: Hiking around Patones offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Lozoya and Jarama rivers, featuring lush forests and dramatic limestone mountains. The region is characterized by river valleys, cliffs, and the prominent El Atazar Lake. Trails often traverse geological formations like the Barranco de Patones, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Patones The most popular hiking route is Cancho de la Cabeza via Arroyo de Patones Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views of El Atazar Lake. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pontón de la Oliva to La Parra Dam Circular Trail, a moderate 10.6 miles (17.0 km) path that explores the area around the historic Pontón de la Oliva dam and the Lozoya river. Local hikers also love the Cañada de la Caleriza Trail loop from Pontón de la Oliva, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain near the Lozoya river, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Patones is defined by river valleys, limestone mountains, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Patones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Patones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Patones?
A: Patones offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 50 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to different preferences and skill levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Patones?
A: The trails in Patones range from easy strolls to more demanding ascents. You'll find approximately 13 easy routes, 33 moderate trails, and 6 more difficult options, providing diverse experiences for all hikers.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Patones?
A: Yes, Patones has several family-friendly trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17508190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada de la Caleriza Trail loop from Pontón de la Oliva&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.2 km route that can be a good option for active families. The El Barranco Ecological Trail, connecting Patones de Abajo with Patones de Arriba, is also known for being family-friendly and showcases the area's geological beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Patones?
A: Many of the trails in Patones are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956893847/ponton-de-la-oliva-to-la-parra-dam-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva to La Parra Dam Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 17 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022912375/carcavas-de-valdepenas-de-la-sierra-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cárcavas de Valdepeñas de la Sierra Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a 14.7 km circular experience.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Patones?
A: Patones is rich in natural beauty, defined by the Lozoya and Jarama rivers. You'll encounter lush river valleys, dense forests, and dramatic limestone mountains. The prominent El Atazar Lake offers impressive panoramic views, especially from elevated points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The Barranco de Patones is also a significant geological feature with landscapes sculpted by water.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore near the trails?
A: Absolutely. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4606981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patones de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; is a major attraction itself, known for its unique 'black architecture' built from local slate. You can also visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam&lt;/a&gt;, an old dam with cliffside pathways, and the Reguerillo Cave, an archaeological site with Stone Age engravings.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Patones?
A: The hiking routes in Patones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 220 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning views, particularly from high points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Patones offers more difficult routes. The climb to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt; is a rewarding ascent, often undertaken as part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957364916/cancho-de-la-cabeza-via-arroyo-de-patones-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza via Arroyo de Patones Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking 360-degree views of El Atazar Lake and the Sierra de Ayllón.

Q: Is Patones accessible by public transport for hikers?
A: While Patones is a popular hiking destination, public transport options can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance, especially for connections to Patones de Arriba, as services may be infrequent. Many visitors choose to drive to the area.

Q: Where can I find parking when hiking in Patones?
A: Parking is generally available in Patones de Abajo and near the access points for popular trails, such as those around Pontón de la Oliva. However, during peak seasons or weekends, parking spaces can fill up quickly, especially in the vicinity of Patones de Arriba. Arriving early is recommended.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or cooling off near the trails?
A: The river valleys, particularly along the Lozoya and Jarama rivers, offer refreshing routes with opportunities to cool off in crystalline water pools. These areas are known for their natural beauty and can provide a welcome respite during warmer months.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Patones?
A: Patones is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for hiking, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher trails might require appropriate gear.
tips: The La Parra dam was built with the purpose of relieving the Navarejos dam, which is located downstream of the Lozoya River. Due to more recent works, today the dam is in disuse but forms part of the well-known La Parra Dam - Pontón de la Oliva route.

The Pontón de la Oliva dam, built during the reign of Isabel II, is the oldest dam in Madrid. It is built in limestone, the rock that we find in the surroundings.
Since the mid-eighteenth century there was already the idea of supplying water to the court from some of the mighty rivers of the Sierra de Madrid, such as the Lozoya. It is in the middle of the 19th century, when the olive pontoon was selected as the most suitable place to build the first dam in Madrid.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The Pontón de la Oliva to La Parra Dam Circular Trail usually takes between 4.5 to 6.5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and potentially some muddy sections.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available near the starting point of the trail, often in the vicinity of the Pontón de la Oliva Dam. Specific parking areas may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, and there are no entrance fees to hike the Pontón de la Oliva to La Parra Dam Circular Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, including well-defined trails, single-tracks, and forest tracks. Some sections may be paved, and you should be prepared for moderate ascents and descents. There's also a mention of a 'slippery firebreak' that requires careful navigation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features, including the historic Pontón de la Oliva Dam and Cliffside Pathways, the disused La Parra Weir, and the scenic Azud de Navarejos with its clear waters. The trail also follows parts of the historic Canal de la Parra, where you can see 'attack tunnels' carved into rock walls.

faqs.question: Are there any other well-known paths that intersect with this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Senderos del Jarama and Senda 2: Remontando el Lozoya hasta la Presa de la Parra. You'll also find sections that align with the GR-88 and GR-10 blazes, which aid in navigation.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: While generally considered moderate, some parts of the trail are described as easy-medium. It requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness due to varied terrain and some ascents/descents. For families with younger children or beginners, it might be a challenging full-day hike, but shorter sections could be more manageable.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is primarily a natural hiking experience without direct facilities. However, you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in nearby towns such as Patones de Arriba or other villages in the Sierra Norte de Madrid region.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection, and layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also recommended, although the trail is generally well-marked with GR-88 and GR-10 blazes.

waypoints.name: Pontón de la Oliva Dam and Cliffside Pathways
